title: AMK ScheduleConfig
description: THE LOOP-2 EDIT SURFACE. The structured object a coding agent proposes; the frozen AMK VM deterministically lowers it into a runnable, validated megakernel. The agent NEVER writes kernel code or touches Task.sm directly -- it only chooses a point in this space, and schedule.ir.validate() turns any unsafe point into a clean REJECTED rather than a hung GPU. This schema mirrors schedule.ir.ScheduleConfig (the dataclass is the source of truth; this JSON Schema documents the editable surface, types, and ranges for an external agent). Unknown extra keys are dropped by MegakernelProgram.from_dict for additive compatibility.

tiling.description: Tile sizes per op archetype. Only 'gemv'->'N_tile' is consumed by the current frozen lowerer (the GEMV output-column tile width); 'attention'->'kv_block' is reserved/searchable and recorded but not yet lowered. Larger N_tile = fewer, wider tiles (less sync, more registers); smaller = more parallelism. The lowerer clamps N_tile to [1, N] and widens it if a projection would exceed 64 tiles.

N_tile.description: GEMV output-column tile width. Typical search choices: 64, 128, 256, 512. Default 256.

kv_block.description: KV window block size for attention tiling. Reserved/searchable; recorded in the schedule but not yet consumed by the frozen lowerer (whole-window attention). Typical: 64, 128, 256. Default 128.

fusion_grouping.description: Lists of op-name groups to fuse into a single resident task group. Each inner list is one fused group (e.g. ['gate','up'] to co-resident the two MLP up-projections, or ['gate','up','silu'] to also fuse the SwiGLU activation). [] = no fusion (the default). Recorded on the schedule; the frozen lowerer treats fusion as a hint and never lets it create an invalid (deadlocking/racing) program.

sm_assignment.description: SM assignment policy: 'round_robin' or 'load_balance' (string policies the lowerer/loader resolve into each Task.sm over the global topological order), OR an explicit {task_id: sm} map for full manual control. INPUT only -- the agent never edits Task.sm; the frozen lowerer/loader resolves this. An explicit map that violates per-SM serial-queue DAG ordering is REJECTED by validate().

pipelining_depth.description: Software-pipelining depth: how many instructions ahead to prefetch weights, hiding the inter-op HBM bubble (the biggest megakernel win). 0 = no prefetch. Typical search choices: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4. Default 2.

page_allocation.description: Activation page-allocation policy. 'graph_color' (default) reuses one physical page across non-overlapping activation live ranges (lowest scratch footprint); 'linear' gives every activation its own page (simplest, more scratch); 'none' disables the allocator. Page reuse with overlapping live ranges is a clobber race and is REJECTED by validate().

threads_per_block.description: Block size for the persistent VM kernel. Must be a multiple of the warp size (32). Typical search choices: 128, 256, 512. The loader proves occupancy (max co-resident blocks > 0) against the target before launch; a config that yields zero occupancy fails cleanly at load.

smem_bytes_per_block.description: Dynamic shared-memory opt-in per block, in bytes. MUST be <= the target's smem_bytes_per_block_optin (e.g. 101376 on rtx5090). 0 = no dynamic SMEM. The loader REJECTS a value above the target opt-in cap before launch. Typical: 0, 16384, 49152.

kernel_knobs.description: Optional GEMV-kernel build knobs threaded to MegakernelVM(knobs=...) when the program is realized on hardware. A config WITHOUT kernel_knobs is byte-identical to the production VM build (the incumbent). These move MEASURED latency (device=cuda); the analytic/CPU 'predicted' path does not model them. Unknown sub-keys are dropped and missing ones filled with the production default. Not required.

cols_per_warp.description: Output columns one warp computes -- x-reuse / memory-level parallelism in GEMV. Default 1.

cpasync.description: 1 = cp.async double-buffered GEMV (latency-hiding); 0 = register/coalesced path. Default 1.

cpa_stages.description: cp.async ring depth -- deeper pipeline hides more HBM latency (must fit SMEM). Default 4.

cpa_cols.description: Columns one warp streams at once under cp.async. Default 2.
